content: Sam, 31, backend/ML engineer at a Paris logistics startup — session 2026-05-16. Reversal of decision from 2026-03-07: we are switching Cairn from React Native to Flutter because of map rendering jank that we can't reliably fix in RN. Concrete facts: Cairn is used by drivers and planners; RN codebase ~45k LOC JS, ~120 modules, 12 native modules (custom map overlays, offline routing C++ lib, BLE, background location). Problem: map freezes 200–400 ms on panning/zooming, polyline updates drop frames during 1s GPS bursts, CPU spikes on mid devices. I want a migration plan with milestones, owners, timeline and rollback/measurement criteria. Target: finish migration and staged rollout before the next Q3 milestone — realistic deadline I’m thinking is 2026-08-01. Team: Jules (mobile lead), Marion (mobile), Léa (QA), me (Sam) backend/CI. Budget: can hire a contractor for plugin work (~€8,000) if needed. Start from this reversal and sketch the plan.
Got it. Quick clarifying question: which platforms must be supported at cutover — iOS and Android only, or web/desktop too?

iOS and Android first, web later. Must preserve feature parity: offline vector tiles, the existing C++ offline routing, background location and BLE, push notifications, 1s GPS updates, and custom vehicle markers. Performance targets: map re-render <100 ms on route changes, stable 45+ FPS on mid devices, memory <150 MB, CPU <15% on Pixel- or iPhone-mid class. Plan outline I’m imagining: prototype -> core infra -> feature parity -> QA & rollout. Timeline proposal: prototype 2 weeks (2026-05-16 to 2026-05-30), core infra 3 weeks (to 2026-06-20), feature parity 4 weeks (to 2026-07-18), QA & polish 1 week (to 2026-07-25) — but this assumes we can reuse the C++ lib via FFI and that map SDK integration is straightforward.
Sharp question: do you intend to reuse the existing C++ routing library via FFI (so keep one native routing implementation), or rebuild routing as a Dart plugin?

Plan is to reuse the C++ routing lib via FFI — we already ship it as .so/.a for RN native modules. That means changes to the Android NDK/CMake build in CI and adding iOS static lib targets for Flutter. For maps we’ll use Mapbox native SDKs because of vector tiles and offline support; Flutter’s mapbox_gl is incomplete so we will fork + patch. Concrete adjustments: allocate 1 week to fork + 2 weeks to implement Dart plugin wrappers for Mapbox native SDKs. That pushes feature parity by +1 week. New realistic end date becomes 2026-08-01.
Okay. Important risk question: what’s the rollback trigger and timing — on what date do you decide to stop migration and fall back to RN if performance targets aren’t met?

Rollback plan: hard stop/decision point 2026-07-20. If by 2026-07-20 automated benchmarks and device tests don't meet targets (45 FPS stable on mid devices, map re-render <100 ms, CPU <15%), we pause cutover and keep RN as production with a freeze on new mobile features for 4 weeks while we either fix Flutter or invest in RN-native fixes. Operationally: maintain both branches; keep RN hotfixes (Marion 40% time) while Jules goes full-time on migration. Perf measurement: bench on 3 device tiers — low (Moto E), mid (Pixel 5a or equivalent), high (iPhone 12). Run automated perf harness simulating 10k route updates and collect FPS, CPU, memory, render latencies. Staged rollout if pass: 10% -> 50% -> 100% over 2–3 weeks starting 2026-08-02.

Short question: do you have capacity to keep RN production-ready for the 4-week parallel-maintenance window (Marion 40%, Jules focused on migration, plus your backend/CI support) or do you need to hire extra help for RN hotfixes?

Capacity is tight. Team plan: Jules full-time on migration, Marion 60/40 (60% RN maintenance, 40% migration) initially but will flip to 40/60 after the prototype. I’ll handle backend/CI changes and build pipelines. Hire a contractor for 2 weeks to accelerate the Mapbox plugin work — cost €8,000 — and Léa will invest 1.5 weeks on building the perf automation. Budget approval required by 2026-05-22 to onboard contractor. Communication: notify ops and driver team 2026-06-01, update runbooks for background-location and battery impact, and prepare a rollback playbook.

Reflection: main milestones now — prototype done by 2026-05-30, core infra by 2026-06-20, plugin + feature parity by 2026-07-25, decision point 2026-07-20, staged rollout from 2026-08-02 to full by ~2026-08-23. Final sharp ask: do you approve contractor hire (€8,000) and will you request budget sign-off by 2026-05-22?
